finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_15.md:141 — 'just a bad week. You get your money back, Christmas is normal, done' replaces 'temporary liquidity issue' with family/gambling register and seasonal specificity; chapters/chapter_15.md:215 — 'I've been coming to you with this same shit since I was twenty' replaces 'curated a strong brand' with relational, age-specific, profane self-awareness rooted in the brother dynamic; chapters/chapter_15.md:373 — 'You're being a prick about it and it's not helping' replaces 'not helping the overall vibe' with direct, period-neutral complaint; chapters/chapter_15.md:401 — 'are we calling it a loss' replaces 'medically compromised' with gambling vocabulary applied to frozen peas, distinctly Tommy's register
finding_results.notes: All four cited Tommy lines reworked to draw from 2005 gambling, bar, family, and neighborhood language. 'Temporary liquidity issue' became a stacked domestic pitch ending on 'Christmas is normal, done' — Tommy selling normalcy in family terms. 'Curated a strong brand' became 'coming to you with this same shit since I was twenty' — relational, age-specific, profane in Tommy's high-contraction register. 'Overall vibe' removed entirely in favor of direct concrete complaint. 'Medically compromised' became 'calling it a loss' — gambling language applied to frozen peas, a joke only Tommy would make in this book. Supporting narrator comparison at line 143 adjusted from 'wearing a headset and pitching venture capital' to 'sell the wreckage back to you as a plan that was already working' so Nate's 2021 interior comparison still tracks without claiming Tommy's actual words sound like VC language. None of the revised lines could plausibly be reassigned to Nate's interior voice. All retain Tommy's humor and desperation.

summary: Single finding (chapter_15_f02) fully addressed. Four Tommy dialogue lines and one supporting narrator observation revised to eliminate post-2005 internet/corporate meta-speak ('temporary liquidity issue,' 'curated a strong brand,' 'overall vibe,' 'medically compromised') and replace with 2005-period gambling, family, and neighborhood language that preserves Tommy's humor, desperation, and idiolect separation from Nate. No other chapter text altered. Heading contract preserved. Contraction levels verified against style bible for all speaking characters (Tommy, Nate, Sarah — all high). No style bible example lines appear in the chapter.
